audience:
API data extraction for dummies
Using the API to get data out of REDCap into Excel and Google Sheets
speaker: Dan Foley

type: Best Practices When Designing Data Capture Instruments
audience:
Best Practices When Designing Data Capture Instruments: What are considered best practices? How do you name your variable? How do you create your code set? What field types do you use? This session is about learning from each other and how best we go about when designing a data collection instrument.

(Administrative)
speaker: John Liman and Nick Shortt

audience:
Flexible Data Ingestion into REDCap to streamline and sustain users with recurrent data imports to REDCap. The topic will describe years of experience gained through the development and deployment of in-house, custom-built tool called REDCap Data Import, or REDDI, and current development efforts towards a generalizable approach that leads to version REDDI 2.0.

(Administrative)
speaker: Eduardo Morales and Lynn Simpson

audience:
Join us for an overview of REDCap's two mobile applications: REDCap Mobile App and MyCap. During this session we will give an introduction to each app, share uses and utilization statistics, as well as share best practices for REDCap Administrators on supporting each mobile application (project setup, testing, device management, how to train data collection, etc.) and common challenges.

(Administrative)
speaker: Michelle Fernandez, Emily Serdoz, and Stephany Duda

type: Impact of eConsent and Sustainable Support Model
audience:
Impact of eConsent and sustainable support model: During the pandemic, Mass General Brigham launched a 21 CFR Part 11 validated eConsent Workflow to support research studies that required signatures on IRB stamped approved Informed Consent PDFs. eConsent continues to be the most popular REDCap template and workflow. This session will discuss how we continue to reevaluate, update and improve the workflow to sustain support.

(Administrative)
speaker: Lynn Simpson, Dan Foley, and Eduardo Morales

audience:
What are Action Tags? How to use it? List of action tags.

(Administrative)
speaker: John Liman and Nick Shortt

audience:
In this session, attendees will learn about the setup and implementation of REDCap FHIR using the Cerner Millennium EHR. Attendees will learn to create REDCap projects with Clinical Data Mart setup and also Clinical Data Pull setup. Attendees will also learn how to help research projects with FHIR EHR Integration.

(Both Technical & Administrative)
speaker: Vasanthi Mandhadi, Yaswitha Jampani, and Scott Carey

audience:
Connecting REDCap Projects: Review some of the tools available for connecting projects together

Attendees will learn how to use mskPyCap, a Python Class for interacting with REDCap, and go over common use cases.

(Technical)
speaker: Julian Schwartz

type: Using REDCap to Make an Impact on Infectious Disease Control
audience:
Mass General Brigham's REDCap team supports Occupational Health for many different employee initiatives including Flu/P2P/TB vaccination tracking, and COVID/MPX exposure and symptom tracking. A few key external modules and integrations help us manage (urgent) project requests.

(Both Technical & Administrative)
speaker: Dimitar Dimitrov and Lynn Simpson

type: Use of REDCap for Quality Improvement
audience:
Attendees will be able to describe different ways to use REDCap for Quality Improvement including clinical and non-clinical situations. Attendees will participate in open brainstorming and sharing of ways to use/ways they have used REDCap to support quality improvement initiatives.

(Administrative)
speaker: Rachel Wenthe, Laurie Bailey, and Alice Hoeft

audience:
Learn about the data migration tools REDCap-ETL and REDCap Custodian. Learn what each can do and how to get started in each. Learn how to pick the right tool to meet your need.

(Technical)
speaker: Philip Chase, Jim Mullen, and Kyle Chesney

audience:
REDCap in a FISMA environment: cloud strategies, secure research computing environments, and compliant data flow: For the RECOVER (Researching Covid to Enhance Recovery) study (https://recovercovid.org/), Mass General Brigham and Harvard Medical School supports REDCap in a FISMA (Federal Information Security Management Act) Moderate environment. The session will focus on compliance with security requirements, the impact to REDCap features and functionality, and the technical solutions implemented to launch and maintain the study.

(Technical)
speaker: Lynn Simpson, James Weatherell, and Dimitar Dimitrov

audience:
Implementation and governance of Twilio in REDCap

(Both Technical & Administrative)
speaker: Yaswitha Jampani and Scott Carey

type: Meeting Research Recruitment needs with REDCap
audience:
Share needs and strategies related to using REDCap to support research recruitment, including how to coordinate recruitment amongst multiple studies.

(Both Technical & Administrative)
speaker: Andy Arenson, Catherine Bauer-Martinez, and Laurie Bailey,

audience:
Data Quality Checks Application: a supplemental Quality Assessment Tool to use with REDCap

Automated Testing of External Modules: tools, strategies and how-tos.

Provide ideas on how we are organized and answer all raised questions. Opportunity to learn from other regional consortia in terms of how they started their regional groups.

audience:
BioSpecimen Tracking: Strategies and Tools. How to use a newly developed external module for BioSpecimen tracking.

(Both Technical & Administrative)
speaker: Leila Deering and Chris Kadolph

audience: (All Attendees)

The instance of REDCap at the US Veterans Health Administration is unique, which has implications for collaborative REDCap projects involving the VA. VA REDCap was recently approved for storage of PHI and PII, but it is still an off-the-shelf installation (no plug-ins or hooks) available on the VA network only.

(All Attendees)
speaker: Bonnie Paris

audience:
How we started the respective Regional Consortia. Each group will discuss their respective achievements and the impact of the groups on the REDCap consortium.

(All Attendees)
speaker: Regions Represented: South Africa, Germany, Brazil, Australia, Canada, Spanish-Speaking Countries

type: Reflections on Launching the Yale Study Support Suite
audience:
The Yale Study Support Suite (YES3) is a planned collection of EMs that extend REDCap's functionality with workflow support tools, based on experience running studies for Yale School of Medicine and the VA. The first of these, the YES3 Exporter, is available now on the EM Repository. We were lucky to receive funding from the NIH Office of Data Science Strategy (ODSS) that had a huge impact, allowing us to grow our program. We will discuss some of the challenges faced by our research software engineering team and how we are approaching them.

(All Attendees)
speaker: Peter Charpentier, Mary Geda, and Maxwell Wibert

audience:
In the US, the Code of Federal Regulations (21 CFR Part 11) and the Federal Information Security Management Act (FISMA), impact how we host and support REDCap for human research studies. This session will compare the two regulations, summarize the effort required for compliance, and review how we prepare our researchers for successful sponsor and government audits.

(All Attendees)
speaker: Lynn Simpson

type: Harmonist Hub: a REDCap External Module for managing research consortia
audience:
The "Harmonist Hub" is a REDCap External Module that functions as a web portal to support the workflow activities of a multi-site research network. We will share our work to develop and generalize this external module for use by other groups.

(All Attendees)
speaker: Stephany Duda
